questions.question: Evaluate \(\int (x^3 + 2)^5 \cdot 3x^2 \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = x^3 + 2\), \(du = 3x^2 dx\). The integral becomes \(\int u^5 du = \frac{u^6}{6} + C = \frac{(x^3 + 2)^6}{6} + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \frac{2x + 1}{x^2 + x + 3} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = x^2 + x + 3\), \(du = (2x + 1) dx\). The integral becomes \(\int \frac{1}{u} du = \ln|u| + C = \ln|x^2 + x + 3| + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int_0^{\pi/2} \sin^3 x \cdot \cos x \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = \sin x\), \(du = \cos x dx\). Limits: \(x=0 \to u=0\), \(x=\pi/2 \to u=1\). Then \(\int_0^1 u^3 du = [\frac{u^4}{4}]_0^1 = \frac{1}{4}\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int x^2 \cdot e^{2x^3} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = 2x^3\), \(du = 6x^2 dx\) so \(x^2 dx = \frac{du}{6}\). Then \(\int e^u \cdot \frac{du}{6} = \frac{1}{6} e^u + C = \frac{1}{6} e^{2x^3} + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \frac{\cos x}{\sin^2 x} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = \sin x\), \(du = \cos x dx\). The integral becomes \(\int u^{-2} du = -u^{-1} + C = -\frac{1}{\sin x} + C = -\csc x + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \tan x \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Rewrite as \(\int \frac{\sin x}{\cos x} dx\). Let \(u = \cos x\), \(du = -\sin x dx\). Then \(\int \frac{-du}{u} = -\ln|u| + C = -\ln|\cos x| + C = \ln|\sec x| + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \frac{x}{\sqrt{4 - x^2}} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = 4 - x^2\), \(du = -2x dx\) so \(x dx = -\frac{du}{2}\). Then \(\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{u}} \cdot (-\frac{du}{2}) = -\frac{1}{2} \int u^{-1/2} du = -\frac{1}{2} \cdot 2u^{1/2} = -\sqrt{u} + C = -\sqrt{4 - x^2} + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int (x + 1) \cdot \sin(x^2 + 2x) \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = x^2 + 2x\), \(du = (2x + 2) dx = 2(x + 1) dx\) so \((x + 1) dx = \frac{du}{2}\). Then \(\int \sin u \cdot \frac{du}{2} = -\frac{1}{2} \cos u + C = -\frac{1}{2} \cos(x^2 + 2x) + C\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int_1^4 \frac{e^{\sqrt{x}}}{\sqrt{x}} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = \sqrt{x}\), \(du = \frac{1}{2\sqrt{x}} dx\) so \(\frac{1}{\sqrt{x}} dx = 2 du\). Limits: \(x=1 \to u=1\), \(x=4 \to u=2\). Then \(\int_1^2 e^u \cdot 2 du = 2[e^u]_1^2 = 2(e^2 - e)\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\int \frac{x}{x^4 + 1} \, dx\)
questions.explanation: Let \(u = x^2\), \(du = 2x dx\) so \(x dx = \frac{du}{2}\). Then \(\int \frac{1}{u^2 + 1} \cdot \frac{du}{2} = \frac{1}{2} \arctan u + C = \frac{1}{2} \arctan(x^2) + C\).

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered intermediate u-substitution. Ready for more challenging integrals!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the concepts well. Review the ones you missed.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on recognizing u and du in more complex expressions.
scoreMessages.0: Review u-substitution fundamentals and practice identifying the correct substitution.
